query: What happens when you call methods inside Vue templates?

competitors.label: Computed Properties
competitors.selectionReason: This is the canonical Vue explanation of computed caching versus methods and the key difference that methods are re-invoked on every render.
theirStrengths: The official guide is authoritative on computed caching versus method execution semantics.
theirGaps: It is lighter on the production pitfalls of side effects, unstable references, and per-item template method calls inside lists.

realWorldUseCases: A component can re-render for many reasons
a method used inside v-for can run per item per render.
followUpConfusion: the result is not cached like a computed property.
if you’d be annoyed seeing it run on every render in a profiler, make it a computed

realWorldUseCases: The docs explain the caching distinction well, but our page is more explicit about the production costs that show up when template methods are expensive or side-effectful.
actionableExamples: The official examples remain strong enough that the example criterion stays tied.
followUpConfusion: Our answer is clearer on the likely follow-up that reactive reads inside the method are tracked but the returned result itself is still recomputed every render.

actionableExamples: Add one tiny list example later that makes the per-item rerun cost visible at a glance.

competitors.winReason: The official guide is authoritative on computed versus methods, but our page is stronger because it names the exact profiler and correctness problems caused by template method calls.

competitors.label: Vue Interview Questions
competitors.selectionReason: GreatFrontEnd's Vue interview bank is directly relevant because computed properties, template expressions, and performance trade-offs are common Vue interview topics.
theirStrengths: It is a strong interview-prep hub for Vue rendering and computed-versus-method trade-offs.
theirGaps: The category page is broader than this exact rerun-on-every-render question.

realWorldUseCases: performance/correctness pitfalls (expensive work, unstable references, side effects)
followUpConfusion: Anything you reference in a Vue template is evaluated during the component’s render.
When it’s OK to use a method in the template

realWorldUseCases: The bank is relevant for Vue fundamentals, but it is less focused on this exact render-time rerun behavior.
actionableExamples: GreatFrontEnd remains competitive on example-driven interview material, so the example criterion stays tied.
followUpConfusion: The landing page is less explicit about the non-cached result, list-multiplied cost, and side-effect hazards of template method calls.

actionableExamples: Keep the computed-versus-method rule of thumb visible so the answer stays practical rather than purely theoretical.

competitors.winReason: GreatFrontEnd is useful as a Vue interview hub, but our page is stronger because it answers the exact runtime and performance consequences of calling methods during render.

competitors.label: Vue Methods
competitors.selectionReason: Vue School has a topic-direct lesson on template methods and when template expressions should be extracted into methods instead of kept inline.
theirStrengths: It is directly about methods in templates and the role they play in Vue component templates.
theirGaps: It is lighter on rerender cost, list amplification, and the explicit computed-versus-method performance trade-off.

realWorldUseCases: If you call a method in the template, it’s executed again even when the method’s “inputs” didn’t change.
followUpConfusion: The method runs again on every subsequent re-render of that component.

realWorldUseCases: The lesson covers methods well, but our page is stronger because it connects template methods directly to rerender cost and correctness pitfalls.
actionableExamples: Its direct lesson format keeps the example criterion competitive, so it stays tied.
followUpConfusion: Our answer is clearer on the likely follow-up that computed values cache by dependency while methods are re-run every render.

actionableExamples: If needed later, add one unstable-reference example where a template method returns a fresh object each render.

competitors.winReason: Vue School is directly on topic, but our page is stronger because it explains not just what methods are, but what breaks when they are used carelessly in render paths.
